commit.message:
fix: Infobox rechts ausgerichtet, Rahmen passt sich Schriftgröße an

Problem 1 – Position:
  panelX war fix auf ImageWidth * 0.04f (links) gesetzt.
  Neu: Panel wird rechtsbündig positioniert.
  Rechter Rand = ImageWidth * 0.04f (4 % Seitenabstand).
  panelX = ImageWidth - rightMargin - panelWidth

Problem 2 – Feste Breite:
  panelWidth war unveränderlich auf 340f gesetzt.
  Neu: g.MeasureString() misst für jede Zeile die tatsächliche
  Textbreite (Titel-Font bzw. Info-Font getrennt). Die maximale
  Breite bestimmt panelWidth = maxTextWidth + 2 * padding.
  Damit passt der Rahmen sich automatisch an FontSizeBase an.

Weitere Verbesserungen:
  - Einheitlicher innerer Abstand (padding = 16f) für alle Zeilen
  - Frühes Return wenn lines.Count == 0 (kein leerer Rahmen)
  - Entfernung der -10-Offsets (Inkonsistenz zwischen Fill und Draw)

commit.message:
Add validation and unit tests AffineGrid (#27572)

### Description
<!-- Describe your changes. -->
This pull request improves the robustness and correctness of the
`AffineGrid` operator in ONNX Runtime by adding stricter input
validation and updating the implementation for better type safety. It
also introduces comprehensive unit tests to verify error handling for
invalid inputs.

### Input validation enhancements

* Added explicit checks in `affine_grid.cc` to ensure that the batch
dimension in `size` matches the batch dimension in `theta`, and that
`theta` has the correct shape for both 2D (`[N, 2, 3]`) and 3D (`[N, 3,
4]`) cases. This prevents silent errors due to shape mismatches.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L116-R126)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L131-R147)

### Type safety and code clarity improvements

* Updated grid generation functions to use the template type `T` for
Eigen vector creation, replacing hardcoded `float` types. This ensures
correct behavior for different data types and improves code clarity.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L31-R36)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L50-R60)
* Added clarifying comments to matrix and array initializations in
affine grid generator functions to make the code easier to understand
and maintain.

### Test coverage improvements

* Added a series of unit tests in `affine_grid_test.cc` to validate
input shape checks, including cases for incorrect tensor dimensions,
mismatched batch sizes, and invalid `size` lengths. These tests ensure
that the operator fails gracefully with informative error messages when
given invalid inputs.
* Ensured the required test utility header is included to support new
tests.

### Code cleanup

* Removed unused includes from `affine_grid.cc` to tidy up the codebase.

* feat: recursive agent discovery and subdirectory-aware CWD resolution

- Agent discovery now recurses into workspace subdirectories (up to 4 levels)
  to find .github/agents/ and .copilot/agents/ directories, matching the
  existing squad discovery behavior
- CWD resolver now derives project root from agent file path instead of

  CWD (c:\workspace\alfred)

* fix: use cross-platform path separator handling in deriveAgentProjectRoot

path.dirname() on Linux doesn't recognize Windows backslash separators,
returning '.' for paths like 'C:\Users\...\agent'. Use the already-normalized
forward-slash path to find the last separator instead, preserving the
original separator style via substring.

  .copilot/agents/ are discovered alongside .github/agents/.

  activation. Recursive subdirectory scanning is async-only via
  discoverAgentsInWorkspaceAsync used during refresh.

commit.message:
fix(dj): sync button matches BPM only, no longer seeks/resets position

The sync button was calling phaseAlign() which seeks the track to align
beat phases between decks. This caused the track to jump back to the
nearest beat boundary (often position 0), making crossfading impossible.

Now sync only adjusts pitch/BPM to match the other deck, letting the DJ
control positioning manually for smooth crossfades.

fix: add root package.json to partner-portal builder stage

npm workspaces requires the root package.json to resolve the
-w packages/shared-types workspace flag. Without it the build
fails with ENOENT on /app/package.json.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(android): fix USB read timeout — 20ms was too short for PS2000 response

readBytes() was passing stepMs=20 as the bulkTransfer call timeout after
the readTimeout refactor. The PS2000B needs up to ~100ms to process a
query and send a response; 20ms caused all reads to time out silently,
returning empty data.

Fix:
- Use callMs=200 as the per-bulkTransfer timeout (device has time to respond)
- Use stepMs=10 for loop pacing
- Elapsed counter now advances by callMs+stepMs per iteration (conservative)
- queryObject timeout raised from 300ms to 800ms for USB round-trip margin
- If readDeviceInfo() returns zero nominals, emit error and close (instead
  of continuing with broken all-zero readings)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dj): eliminate clicks in fader LFO and scratch pattern chops

Replace abrupt setValueAtTime() gain jumps with 1.5ms linear ramps
at every 0→1 and 1→0 transition. Applies to all four fader schedulers:
- Generic fader LFO (1/4, 1/8, 1/16, 1/32 divisions)
- Transformer pattern (4 chops/beat, 40% duty)
- Crab pattern (4 taps/beat, 28% duty)
- 8-finger crab (8 taps/beat, 20% duty)

The 1.5ms ramp is short enough to preserve the tight chop character
while eliminating the discontinuity clicks at each gain transition.

commit.message:
add machine info script that runs before-ish the pipeline (#347)

* Add comprehensive machine information diagnostic script

- Create get_machine_information.ps1 with detailed diagnostics:
  OS/system info, processor, memory, physical disks with NVMe
  detection, disk-to-volume mapping, logical volumes, software
  versions, and build environment variables
- Update get_machine_information.yml to call script file instead
  of inline code
- Insert machine info template into build_and_run_tests.yml so
  all consuming repos get diagnostics automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move machine information to first step after checkout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make machine information script never fail the pipeline

- Set ErrorActionPreference to SilentlyContinue in both the
  script and the YAML task to suppress non-terminating errors
- Force exit 0 at the end of the script to guarantee success

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add NVMe drive mapping via NTFS junctions before checkout

Create map_nvme_drives.yml that runs before checkout: self to
redirect source and binaries directories to the best available
NVMe drive using NTFS junctions. The agent writes to the default
paths (e.g. D:\a\_work\1\s) but data transparently lands on NVMe.

Falls back gracefully to default paths if no NVMe drives are found
or if junction creation fails. Never fails the pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make machine info inline and add to CodeQL template

- Convert get_machine_information.yml to inline script so it works
  before checkout/submodule init (no file dependency)
- Remove repo_root parameter (no longer needed)
- Add machine info as first step in codeql3000_default.yml
- Reorder build_and_run_tests.yml: machine info -> NVMe map -> checkout
- Show all logical volumes (removed DriveType=3 filter to include D:)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove NVMe drive mapping from pipeline templates

NVMe drives must come pre-provisioned at the agent/image level,
not mapped at pipeline runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add remap_nvme_drive.yml to assign largest NVMe to D:

Inline PowerShell template that finds the largest healthy NVMe
partition, removes its current drive letter, and reassigns it
to D: — only if D: does not already exist. Best-effort, never
fails the pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add NVMe remap before machine info in both pipelines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add physical sector size to machine info disk output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove duplicate machine info from codeql3000_init.yml

It's already called earlier in codeql3000_default.yml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Investigate mystery D: drive with detailed diagnostics

- Machine info: show ALL volumes including 0-size ones, with
  drive type (Local/CD-ROM/Removable/etc.)
- Remap script: when D: exists, print full details (filesystem,
  drive type, health, WMI info) to identify what it is

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remap NVMe to D: even if D: is a CD-ROM or empty volume

If D: exists but is not a local disk with data (e.g. CD-ROM,
empty, removable), free the letter first via partition access
path removal or mountvol, then proceed to assign D: to the
largest NVMe drive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reassign D: to end-of-alphabet letter instead of removing it

When D: is a CD-ROM or non-local drive, move it to Z:/Y:/X:/etc.
instead of dropping its letter entirely, then assign D: to NVMe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Dump .agent config file contents in machine information

Searches known agent install paths for .agent config files
and prints their full contents to help diagnose workFolder
and other agent settings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove NVMe drive letter remapping from pipeline templates

The remap_nvme_drive.yml template and all references to it have been
removed. This approach of remapping NVMe drive letters in pipelines is
not the right way to handle drive letter assignment.

- Deleted pipeline_templates/remap_nvme_drive.yml
- Removed template reference from build_and_run_tests.yml
- Removed template reference from codeql3000_default.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove orphaned get_machine_information.ps1 script

The standalone .ps1 script is unused — the .yml template uses an
inline copy of the same logic. No references to this file exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): remove Sentry wrapper from next.config.ts to restore standalone build

The static 'import { withSentryConfig } from @sentry/nextjs' at module
load time was crashing next.config.ts because serialize-javascript@7.0.3
(npm override from security fix) broke a transitive dep of @sentry/nextjs.

When next.config.ts fails to load, Next.js silently falls back to defaults
(no output: standalone) -> no server.js -> frontend_app crash loop -> 502.

Fix: remove the withSentryConfig wrapper entirely. Sentry still initialises
at runtime via sentry.client.config.ts / sentry.server.config.ts. The
withSentryConfig wrapper was only adding build-time source map upload
optimisations, and was never running in Docker builds anyway since
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN is not set during CI image builds.

The .next/standalone/server.js guard in Dockerfile will catch any regression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: DatabaseAdapter uses access token auth (not ODBC Auth attribute)

The ODBC Driver 18 in the container doesn't support the Authentication=
connection string attribute for AAD. Fixed to use the best-practice
approach: acquire token via DefaultAzureCredential, encode as
UTF-16-LE struct, pass via SQL_COPT_SS_ACCESS_TOKEN (attr 1256).

Changes:
- DatabaseAdapter accepts connection string OR server+database
- Strips any Authentication= clause from connection strings
- Token encoded as struct.pack('<I{len}s', len, bytes) per pyodbc spec
- Added create_sql_user.py script for granting MI database access

through its external interfaces (mock transport + mock handler).

- Protocol and communication (T-0100 to T-0106)
- Command set (T-0200 to T-0205, including priority ordering)

- Application data and handler API (T-0500 to T-0513)
- Authentication and security (T-0600 to T-0609)
- Node management (T-0700 to T-0704)
- Operational (T-1000 to T-1004)

node helper, test handler) and full test-to-requirement traceability
matrix in Appendix A.

- GW-0803: schedule and commands (set schedule, queue reboot/ephemeral)

* Change exports to functions to avoid runtime dependency on @eslint/css-tree

- Convert tailwind3 and tailwind4 exports from objects to SyntaxExtensionCallback functions
- Functions receive prev (base SyntaxConfig) parameter and return extended config
- This allows accessing prev.types and prev.properties without importing from @eslint/css-tree/definition-syntax-data
- Works with pnpm strict dependency resolution
- Updated eslint.config.js to call functions with base config for linting
- All tests pass, no breaking changes to API (fork() accepts both objects and functions)

Co-authored-by: nzakas <38546+nzakas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge origin/main and resolve conflicts

- Merged main branch which added opacity modifier support for Tailwind classes
- Kept function-based exports (SyntaxExtensionCallback) for pnpm compatibility
- Integrated new type definitions: tw-utility-with-variant and tw-utility-with-opacity
- Added opacity parsing logic in tailwind-class.js to handle slash notation (e.g., bg-red-500/50)
- Added comments in tailwind-apply.js for clarity
- Accepted all new tests from main (112 tests now, up from 98)
- All tests pass, linting and formatting applied

Co-authored-by: nzakas <38546+nzakas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert formatting-only changes to non-functional files

Reverted files that were only reformatted (spaces to tabs, trailing whitespace) without functional changes:
- .github/workflows/nodejs-test.yml
- README.md
- src/atrule/tailwind-apply.js
- src/atrule/tailwind-import.js
- src/node/tailwind-class.js

- tests/fixtures/tailwind4.css
- tests/helpers/theme-function-tests.js
- tests/tailwind3.test.js
- tests/tailwind4.test.js

Kept functional changes in:
- src/tailwind3.js (function-based export)
- src/tailwind4.js (function-based export)
- eslint.config.js (updated to work with function exports)

All 112 tests still pass, linting passes

Co-authored-by: nzakas <38546+nzakas@users.noreply.github.com>

- Updated @eslint/css from ^0.8.1 to ^1.0.0

commit.message:
feat: subtle Liquid Ether animated background on all pages

body::after — three slow-drifting radial orbs (violet, amber, blue)
with very low opacity (5-7%). Animated via liquidEther keyframe over
28s ease-in-out infinite alternate: gentle scale + translate drift.

z-index: 0 keeps it below noise texture (1) and all content (2+).
position: fixed so it covers the viewport on scroll without layout shift.
will-change: opacity, transform for GPU compositing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: centralize test credential constants into testing::credentials (#829)

* refactor: centralize test credential constants into testing::credentials

Scattered test credential strings (API keys, OAuth tokens, crypto keys,
Telegram tokens, session tokens) across ~25 files made security auditing
harder and created unnecessary duplication. Centralize all test-only fake
credentials into a new `src/testing/credentials.rs` module with named
constants and a shared `test_secrets_store()` helper.

- Convert `src/testing.rs` to directory module (`src/testing/mod.rs`)
- Add `src/testing/credentials.rs` with ~30 named constants
- Replace hardcoded literals in 24 source files
- Deduplicate `test_store()` helper (was copy-pasted in 3 files)
- Leave leak_detector/shell/signature tests as-is (inline values
  aid readability for pattern detection tests)

[skip-regression-check]
